evals.prompt: Continue the skill creation process. The target directory is /tmp/forged-resume.

expected_output.description: Skill imports existing .skill-plan.yaml via import-plan.sh, correctly determines turn progress, then proceeds from the next unprompted turn. Decision log printed. Asks user about the current turn's question.
assertions: Output contains turn number matching one past turns[] length in .skill-plan.yaml
Skill prints summary: intent, tier, turns completed, target path
Skill asks a question corresponding to the current turn (not an already-answered turn)
No regression: already-answered questions are not repeated

evals.prompt: Create a new skill called 'test-analyzer' that reviews test coverage for a given codebase. It should analyze coverage gaps, suggest missing test scenarios, and classify bugs by severity. The skill will need scripts for analysis and reference checklists.

expected_output.description: Skill-forge produces a Tier B bundle: SKILL.md, scripts/coverage_analyzer.sh, references/Example.md, references/coverage-checklist.md. SKILL.md contains progressive dialogue tree with all turns. .skill-plan.yaml has completed:true.
assertions: SKILL.md has YAML frontmatter with name, description, use_when
SKILL.md contains state machine with turns 0-5
SKILL.md has <= 80 instruction lines, each imperative verb-first
scripts/ contains at least one script file (Tier B requires scripts)
references/ contains Example.md (Tier B placement rule)
.skill-plan.yaml has completed: true
Example.md shows end-to-end transcript with at least one backtrack scenario

evals.prompt: I want to make a skill. Actually wait, scratch that - I want a skill for debugging HTTP APIs, not a general skill.

expected_output.description: Skill detects contradiction between previous intent (general skill) and new intent (HTTP API debugging). Invalidates fork point, asks user to re-confirm intent from Turn 1. Does NOT proceed to Turn 2 with conflicting intent.
assertions: Output flags contradiction between prior and new intent
Output resets turns[] to 0 or clears downstream decisions
Output re-prompts for Turn 1 (intent) rather than skipping ahead
Decision log records the backtrack event

evals.prompt: Create a skill called 'doc-summarizer' that summarizes API documentation. Single task only (Tier A). Just write the SKILL.md.

expected_output.description: Skill-forge drafts SKILL.md (Tier A, no scripts), scans it against anti-patterns, reports violations, fixes them, shows corrected draft. Example embedded inline as ## Example section within SKILL.md.
assertions: SKILL.md avoids all listed anti-patterns: no hedging words, no 'consider/might/perhaps', no >3 sentence paragraphs, no nested lists >2 levels
Every instruction in SKILL.md starts with imperative verb
No instruction exceeds 2 lines
Example section is embedded inline (Tier A placement rule)
Total instruction lines <= 80
Anti-pattern violations file lists any remaining items or states 'all clear'

expected_output.description: Skill detects ambiguous/stuck user input (no concrete intent). Offers 2-3 starter templates from references/Examples.md or built-in defaults. Does NOT proceed with guessed intent.
assertions: Output explicitly asks user to define intent or choose a template
Output provides at least 2 starter templates or examples
Output does NOT guess or assume the user's intent
Output stays at Turn 1 (Onboarding) without proceeding
